summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the image of (5, âˆ’2) after the translation âŸ¨âˆ’3, 4âŸ©?

Substitute the input value into the function expression and simplify using order of operations. The result is (2, 2). The other options — '(8, âˆ’6)', '(2, âˆ’6)', and '(âˆ’3, 4)' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

When composing two translations âŸ¨2, 5âŸ© and âŸ¨âˆ’1, 3âŸ©, what is the resulting translation vector?

Translation is protein synthesis at the ribosome, where mRNA codons are read and matched with tRNA anticodons carrying specific amino acids. The answer is 'âŸ¨1, 8âŸ©'. The other options — 'âŸ¨3, 2âŸ©', 'âŸ¨âˆ’2, 15âŸ©', and 'âŸ¨2, 3âŸ©' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

A triangle is translated by âŸ¨4, âˆ’7âŸ©. What is true about the image triangle?

Translations slide every point the same distance and direction. The shape and size are preserved (rigid motion/isometry). The answer is It is congruent to the original. The other options — 'It is similar but not congruent to the original', 'It has different angle measures', and 'It is larger than the original' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
